spectralAnalysisReadings.entryRule: entry(i,j) is the workflow risk axis score for workflow i and risk axis j

values.name: frobeniusNorm
values.interpretation: global pressure magnitude of the observed finite matrix

values.name: spectralRadiusUpperBound
values.interpretation: nonnegative-matrix upper bound, not an exact eigen theorem

dominantComponents.reading: dominant row pressure in the workflow-by-axis matrix
dominant column pressure accumulated across workflows

spectralAnalysisReadings.reading: workflow risk pressure is concentrated by rows and axes; use the dominant row and column before treating local risk as isolated
spectralAnalysisReadings.coverageBoundary: coverage gaps on workflow axes block measured-zero interpretation for absent entries
spectralAnalysisReadings.zeroReflectingBoundary: zero entries mean no observed risk-axis evidence under current ArchMap and heuristics, not absence of architectural pressure
spectralAnalysisReadings.recommendedNextAction: review the dominant workflow row, dominant axis column, and coverage gaps before selecting repair operations

spectralAnalysisReadings.entryRule: entry(i,j) is exact atom overlap weighted with atom-family overlap; the diagonal records molecule atom-family mass

values.name: frobeniusNorm
values.interpretation: observed overlap magnitude of the molecule coupling matrix

values.name: spectralRadiusUpperBound
values.interpretation: Gershgorin-style row-sum upper bound for this nonnegative matrix

dominantComponents.reading: dominant molecule by atom/family overlap mass

spectralAnalysisReadings.reading: molecules sharing atom refs or atom families form an overlap coupling surface that is invisible to import-only static analysis
spectralAnalysisReadings.coverageBoundary: missing atom families and observation gaps can make overlap coupling appear smaller than the source architecture actually is
spectralAnalysisReadings.zeroReflectingBoundary: zero off-diagonal overlap means no shared observed atom/family evidence, not guaranteed independence
spectralAnalysisReadings.recommendedNextAction: inspect the dominant molecule and any high-overlap neighbors before splitting or moving responsibilities

spectralAnalysisReadings.entryRule: entry(i,j) is 1 when obstruction circuit i contributes to signature axis j

values.name: frobeniusNorm
values.interpretation: curvature incidence magnitude over constructed obstruction circuits

values.name: spectralRadiusUpperBound
values.interpretation: incidence-matrix upper bound, not a global flatness proof

dominantComponents.reading: dominant obstruction row by connected signature axes
dominant curvature column across obstruction circuits

spectralAnalysisReadings.reading: obstruction circuits distribute curvature onto signature axes; repeated columns indicate axes where local failures accumulate
spectralAnalysisReadings.coverageBoundary: only constructed obstruction circuits are represented; concern hints that lack witness rules remain outside this matrix
spectralAnalysisReadings.zeroReflectingBoundary: zero incidence means no constructed obstruction-to-axis edge, not proof of zero curvature
spectralAnalysisReadings.recommendedNextAction: review the dominant obstruction row and repeated axis column before treating a signature axis as local
